issue_body:

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

Keycloak uses a set of the deprecated options and their alternatives, which leads to differently sorted output in the docs.

main

All options should be stable in their sorting. Possibly sorted alphabetically automatically.

STRICT and WILDCARD have been deprecated, use DEFAULT instead.
*Deprecated values: `+WILDCARD+`, `+STRICT+`*

and

STRICT and WILDCARD have been deprecated, use DEFAULT instead.
*Deprecated values: `+STRICT+`, `+WILDCARD+`*

See downstream docs build for RHBK26

issue_title: Clients invalidated on each client credential grant
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

After the change in #34664 a client is invalidated on each call as `client.getClientScopes(true).containsKey(serviceAccountScope.getId())` is always false as the `id` is a UUID and the keys are the scope names.

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/226daa41c72a7c2f0831dcaec6890eeed67afb30/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/grants/ClientCredentialsGrantType.java#L86

### Version

main

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

If a default scope is already set, don't call enable service account

### Actual behavior

KC runs enable service account, thereby invalidating the Keycloak cache

### How to Reproduce?

Debugging
commit_message:
Fix to check correctly that the service account scope is assigned

Closes #34875

Signed-off-by: rmartinc <rmartinc@redhat.com>

issue_title: Upgrade 24 to 25 fails because db jpa changes drop nonexisting indexes.
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

When upgrading from keycloak 24 to keycloak 25 the migration fails because the jpa upgrade script has DROP INDEX instructions from columns who do not exist on PRODUCTION environment only.
THis because keycloak doesn't create indexes if nr of records above a limit. Therefore OTA succeeded.

Its not the first time our upgrade failed and we had to generate sql scripts via other upgrade method...

It whould be handy to check if indexes exist in the jpa instructions, to avoid migration failure.



### Version

24.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Migration doesn't fail if indexes that are dropped in jpa do NOT exist.


### Actual behavior

Failure on drop instructions:

ERROR: Can't DROP 'IDX_OFFLINE_USS_CREATEDON'; check that column/key exists [Failed SQL: (1091) DROP INDEX IDX_OFFLINE_USS_CREATEDON ON keycloak_prod.OFFLINE_USER_SESSION]


the following columns do only exist on our ota environment and NOT on production, since production has records above the limit that keycloak creates indexes:

   <dropIndex tableName="OFFLINE_USER_SESSION" indexName="IDX_OFFLINE_USS_CREATEDON" />
        <dropIndex tableName="OFFLINE_USER_SESSION" indexName="IDX_OFFLINE_USS_PRELOAD" />
        <dropIndex tableName="OFFLINE_USER_SESSION" indexName="IDX_OFFLINE_USS_BY_USERSESS" />
        <dropIndex tableName="OFFLINE_CLIENT_SESSION" indexName="IDX_OFFLINE_CSS_PRELOAD" />
        
        
        




### How to Reproduce?

remove above indexes and upgrade from keycloak 24 to 25..

(othere upgrade versions had this issue before, so therefore report it now).

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Upgrade 24 to 25 fails because db jpa changes drop nonexisting indexes. 

Closes #34899

Signed-off-by: vramik <vramik@redhat.com>

issue_title: Update Email doesn't update username when Email as Username and Attributes are enabled
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area user-profile ### Describe the bug Updating email action fails to update username when `email as username` and `Unmanaged Attributes` are enabled. ### Version 26.0.5 ### Regression - [X] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior When the email is changed, the username is changed as well. User can only login with the new email, but not the old one. ### Actual behavior When the email is changed, the username remains the same. User can login with the new email **and** the old one. ### How to Reproduce? Create a realm with this settings: Set `Unmanaged Attributes` to `ENABLED`: Then try to change the email of a user and you'll see that the username get updated. ### Anything else? Even though the email update feature is in preview, I find it important to have a closer look into this issue, since this level of details AFAIK is not covered by migration guides as far as I can see. It's too easy to do an upgrade an miss this detail, and the consequence is that after the user changes the email, you can still login with the older email (as it remains the username) **and** with the new one. I tracked it down to the `DefaultAttributes` class. Everything is fine until we hit the below line and the `Unmanaged Attributes` is enabled. https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blame/f5b3a1984a5ec99bd5c2e4b1135f039762d7add7/server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/userprofile/DefaultAttributes.java#L428 I put this issue as a **regression** because I don't see a reason why the `Unmanaged Attributes` should interfere here. ## Workaround Set `Unmanaged Attributes` to `DISABLED`.
commit_message:
Make sure unmanaged attributes are populated before updating username when in update email context

Closes #34930

Signed-off-by: Pedro Igor <pigor.craveiro@gmail.com>

issue_title: Adding "sub" claim to lightweight access token causes HTTP 403 Forbidden Error in Keycloak 26.0.5
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

We are encountering a 403 Forbidden error after enabling the “Add to lightweight access token” flag for the sub claim in Keycloak 26.0.5. Despite the sub claim being successfully included in the token (as verified by decoding the access token), the user receives a 403 response when attempting to access Keycloak resources (for example, via the /admin/master/console/whoami API).



### Version

26.0.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

After enabling the "Add to lightweight access token" flag for the sub claim, we expect the sub claim to be present in the lightweight access token and for the request to proceed without encountering a 403 Forbidden error.

### Actual behavior

The sub claim is present in the lightweight access token (as verified by decoding the token).
![Pasted Graphic 1](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a87bd11c-8af9-4e41-8f59-c315da0088ca)

After logging in, a 403 Forbidden error is returned when accessing Keycloak resources (as seen in the logs and browser console).
![Pasted Graphic](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/523c7b45-1ff1-44ee-96f5-34db5fad630f)

Logs show a ForbiddenException when trying to access resources.

This setting should only be enabled when you are certain that the Connections given to Hibernate by the ConnectionProvider have auto-commit disabled.  Enabling this setting when the Connections do not have auto-commit disabled will lead to Hibernate executing SQL operations outside of any JDBC/SQL transaction.

> 2024-11-14 11:29:14,656 DEBUG [org.keycloak.services.error.KeycloakErrorHandler] (executor-thread-15) Error response Forbidden: jakarta.ws.rs.ForbiddenException: HTTP 403 Forbidden
> 	at org.keycloak.services.resources.admin.permissions.RealmPermissions.requireViewRealmNameList(RealmPermissions.java:87)
> 	at org.keycloak.services.resources.admin.RealmAdminResource.getRealm(RealmAdminResource.java:380)
> 	at org.keycloak.services.resources.admin.RealmAdminResource$quarkusrestinvoker$getRealm_1bf39733aab8d3fea8c232249d5424607d2bc915.invoke(Unknown Source)
> 	at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.server.handlers.InvocationHandler.handle(InvocationHandler.java:29)
> 	at io.quarkus.resteasy.reactive.server.runtime.QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.invokeHandler(QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.java:141)
> 	at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.common.core.AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.run(AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.java:147)
> 	at io.quarkus.vertx.core.runtime.VertxCoreRecorder$14.runWith(VertxCoreRecorder.java:635)

### How to Reproduce?

1. In Keycloak 26.0.5, under master realm, click on Client Scopes
2. Click on basic, under Mappers, select claim "sub".
3. Enable the “Add to lightweight access token” flag for the sub claim.
4. Log in to Keycloak providing admin credentials.
5. Observe that a 403 Forbidden error occurs.
6. Decode the access token and verify that the sub claim is present.

issue_title: ES256 key continue to be used to sign token even after expiry
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

I created a java key provider using an ES256 key in the keystore. It works fine and issues access tokens signed by this key.
But even after the key has expired, keycloak continues to use it to sign tokens.
I would have expected, a default ES256 key to be generated and used instead of the expired key.
May be it works like so because KeyCloak does not support ES256 default keys (only RSA256?)

### Version

26.0.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The expired ES256 key should not be used to issue tokens. A new default ES256 key should be generated and used instead or a RSA256 token be issued etc.

### Actual behavior

expired ES256 key continue to be used to sign access tokens.

### How to Reproduce?

Create a java keystore that contains an ES256 key. Make the key expire in 1 day for example.
Use that to create a java key provider and make this a default for a realm.
Wait for the key to expire and login to the realm.
You will see the expired kid still being used.

issue_title: MySQL database migration issue
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

While deploying the update to 26.0.5 (updating from 25.0.6) an unexpected database migration issue was encountered.
This did not happen on my test environment, but it did happen with both production environments.

I believe this has to do with a small collation difference between those two environments: in test my database is `create schema keycloak collate utf8mb4_unicode_ci;`, where the production database use `utf8mb3_unicode_ci`.

However, only three tables are shown to use the mb4 collation and charset in my test environment:

- ORG
- ORG_DOMAIN
- REVOKED_TOKEN

Looking at https://www.keycloak.org/server/db#_configuring_unicode_support_for_a_mysql_database, utf8mb4 is not supported, which would mean that my test environment is running out of spec. But that was the only environment without issues.

Then I found this thread: https://groups.google.com/g/keycloak-user/c/9MhUfXwla3s?pli=1. Looks like `utf8mb4` should be used now with MySQL 8, and that the documentation needs to be updated?


### Version

26.0.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The migration to be completed without issue.

### Actual behavior

```
keycloak-1  | 2024-11-15 13:43:56,257 ERROR [liquibase.changelog.ChangeSet] (main) ChangeSet META-INF/jpa-changelog-26.0.0.xml::26.0.0-org-group::keycloak encountered an exception.: liquibase.exception.DatabaseException: Illegal mix of collations (utf8mb3_general_ci,IMPLICIT) and (utf8mb3_unicode_ci,IMPLICIT) for operation '=' [Failed SQL: (1267) UPDATE keycloak.KEYCLOAK_GROUP SET TYPE = 1 WHERE NAME IN (SELECT ID FROM keycloak.ORG)]
```

```
keycloak-1  | 2024-11-15 13:44:11,230 ERROR [liquibase.changelog.ChangeSet] (main) ChangeSet META-INF/jpa-changelog-26.0.0.xml::26.0.0-org-group::keycloak encountered an exception.: liquibase.exception.DatabaseException: Duplicate column name 'TYPE' [Failed SQL: (1060) ALTER TABLE keycloak.KEYCLOAK_GROUP ADD TYPE INT DEFAULT 0 NULL]
```

### How to Reproduce?

I believe it should be reproducable when upgrading a 25 instance to 26 while using MySQL 8 where the complete database is using the `utf8mb3_unicode_ci` collation and `utf8mb3` character set.

### Anything else?

For now I fixed it by manually marking the migration as completed in the db and adding the `TYPE` column manually. As the specific table was empty, there was no need to run https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/cec081961b7116577dea764adc826b7005b23981/model/jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/connections/jpa/updater/liquibase/custom/JpaUpdate26_0_0_OrganizationGroupType.java manually.

issue_title: Pasword creation date from active directory is wrong
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

ldap

### Describe the bug

AD uses a file time format for storing password creation time, but keycloak probably interprets it as unix time and shows wrong time in account console.

For example my password was created 1.2.2023 at 18:9:49 that is saved in AD as 133197449897147110 whitch is not valid unix time, so account console shows January 1, 1970 at 12:59 AM

I used this tool to convert from file time: [LDAP, Active Directory & Filetime Timestamp Converter](https://www.epochconverter.com/ldap)

### Version

26.0.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Keycloak and AD shows the same password creation time

### Actual behavior

Password creation time when using AD federation is always January 1, 1970 at 12:59 AM

### How to Reproduce?

Add ldap federation from AD.
Then create user-attribute-ldap-mapper from pwdLastSet in AD to pwdLastSet in keycloak, I have enabled Read Only and Always Read Value From LDAP


### Anything else?

I have tried asking on the forum but without success.
https://keycloak.discourse.group/t/pasword-creation-date-from-active-directory-is-wrong/24473

issue_title: PersistentSessionsWorker: retry with 0 backoff ms.
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

We released keycloak 26 and functional and performance wise everything is good.

However around once a day a duplicate insert session happens , which results in 20.000 error messages in a 10 second time frame.

We have reduced this number by overwriting PersistentSessionsWorker, with a back-off time of 100ms instead of "0" (during 5 instead of 10) which is in the code:

   Retry.executeWithBackoff((iteration) -> {
   ....
   }, Duration.of(10L, ChronoUnit.SECONDS), 0);
   
   
   
   The duplicate itself concerns 1 specific session id (1 of the 10.000 ?) around once a day, and doesn't seem to be a functional big issue: 
   
   HHH100501: Exception executing batch [java.sql.BatchUpdateException: Duplicate entry 'b9f91c53-ec37-4aaf-b54d-12f793c58d09-1' for key 'OFFLINE_USER_SESSION.PRIMARY'], SQL: insert into OFFLINE_USER_SESSION (BROKER_SESSION_ID,CREATED_ON,DATA,LAST_SESSION_REFRESH,REALM_ID,USER_ID,VERSION,OFFLINE_FLAG,USER_SESSION_ID) values (?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)
   
   
   (repeated 20.000 times of same entry id.)
   
   
   
   

### Version

26.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

when retry a insert session statement or others, use a backoff time > 0ms to avoid a bulk of same error messages.


### Actual behavior

To many error messages because of back-off delay 0ms.


### How to Reproduce?

generate a sql exception (like duplicate insert).



### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Cannot request additional scopes when using the account console
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

account/api

### Describe the bug

Trying to request additional scopes when authenticating to the account console does not grant the requested scopes. This is a regression introduced by https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/32686.

After the user is successfully authenticated and redirected back to the account console for the very first time, the user is redirected back to the server because it is the first time accessing the account console. At this point, the authorization request does not contain any `scope` parameter, and the scopes previously requested are ignored.

[1] https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/32686

### Version

999-SNAPSHOT

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Any scope requested when changing the `scope` parameter when at the login page needs to be granted.

### Actual behavior

Any scope requested when changing the `scope` parameter at the login page is not granted.

### How to Reproduce?

* Reach the account console at `http://localhost:8180/realms/{realm}/account/`
* At the login page, add/set the `scope` parameter to  `phone` to request this scope
* Authenticate
* Check if the token issued by the server holds the `phone` scope

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: [LoginUI] Set HTML lang attribute to "en" when internationalization disabled
issue_body:
### Description

There are some issues with the current handling of the `lang` attribute in the html tag within the `template.ftl`:

`<html class="${properties.kcHtmlClass!}"<#if realm.internationalizationEnabled> lang="${locale.currentLanguageTag}"</#if>>`

The `lang` attribute is set only when internationalization is enabled, and it is omitted entirely when internationalization is disabled. This behavior could cause problems for screen readers, which rely on the lang attribute for accurate output.

issue_title: reCAPTCHA v3 not working
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

login/ui

### Describe the bug

reCAPTCHA V3 toggle in authentication/registration flow does not seem to be working. 

It always uses reCAPTCHA v2

With v2 keys configured : 
- V3 not toggled =>  It works and shows a v2 captcha checkbox
- V3 toggled =>  It works and shows a v2 captcha checkbox

With v3 keys configured : 
- V3 not toggled =>  It does not work and shows `ERROR for site owner: Invalid key type`
- V3 toggled =>  It does not work and shows `ERROR for site owner: Invalid key type`

### Version

26.0.6

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Invisible reCAPTCHA

### Actual behavior

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4e50d4f6-5eaa-485a-9d9a-0926473c8abd)

Text being : ERROR for site owner: Invalid key type

### How to Reproduce?

Follow steps in https://www.keycloak.org/getting-started/getting-started-docker
=> Allow user registration on the realm

Add reCAPTCHA v3 (Google): 
- authentication/registration flow captcha required
- add v3 keys
- toggle v3 

Realm settings / Security defense 
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3a92d5e8-97a9-4b2d-81fa-0350b3d109eb)

Try to register using `https://www.keycloak.org/app/`

Then you get 

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b2620c90-7b30-4445-a221-4d2bfa6e684b)
 


### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: CONTRIBUTING.md has confusing ordered list with two times point 5
issue_body:

### Before reporting an issue

Set up a new OpenID identity provider and try to authenticate

issue_title: Your login attempt timed out
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

When I do some otp configuration or reset password, my actual password is requested before proceeding.
But after re-inserting the password I get the error:
"Your login attempt timed out.  Login will start from the beginning."

This is confusing users, they did nothing fancy, and they have to start over.

What can be done?




### Version

- [ ] The issue is a regression

After re-typing the password (because too much idle time has passed) I should be brought right to the option I chose (otp configuration or password reset).

Login into keycloak account and go into "Signing in" page.

you are then forced to digit the password before proceeding ("Pleae re-authenticate")

issue_title: Upgrade 26.0.5 -> 26.0.6 completely breaks admin events in the admin UI
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

When upgrading from KC 26.0.5 to 26.0.6 with a PostgreSQL database,
the new 'details_json' column was introduced.
However, when we have some admin events already in there, the column stays NULL.

When already having admin events pre upgrade, the UI completely breaks when opening the event view,
and the REST call to `/admin/realms/master/admin-events?first=0&max=51` returns an error 404 with the message `{"error":"unknown_error","error_description":"Cannot parse the JSON"}`.

Manually executing `UPDATE admin_event_entity SET details_json='{}' WHERE details_json IS NULL;` against the database fixes the view, but the admin events are almost all empty.


### Version

26.0.6

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

A **bugfix** version upgrade should not break the database.

### Actual behavior

A **bugfix** version upgrade broke the database.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Install KC 26.0.5 with PostgreSQL as the database.
2. Enable admin events
3. Generate some admin events (REALM UPDATE and CLIENT CREATE) in our testsystem)
4. Upgrade to KC 26.0.6
5. Try to access the event view, the UI only shows a red error message.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Reverse proxy provided context path not working despite setting X-Forwarded-Prefix header
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

Hello,

Following the deprecation of `hostname:v1`, we are forced to reconfigure how we expose our Keycloak deployments. Due to business requirements, we need to expose the same Keycloak on multiple domains (eg. `https://www.domain.de|it|fr`). 
Therefore, we cannot specify a fixed hostname using the `--hostname` argument but need to resolve it dynamically. 
Moreover, we have to expose the deployment on a specific context path (eg. `/auth`).
This means that Keycloak will be exposed on `https://www.domain.de|it|fr/auth`.
Everything is exposed through an NGINX reverse proxy. With `hostname:v1`, we had a setup that was working using env variables like so:
```
- name: KC_HOSTNAME_STRICT
   value: 'false'
- name: KC_HTTP_ENABLED
   value: 'true'
- name: KC_HOSTNAME_PATH
   value: “/auth/”
- name: KC_HOSTNAME_ADMIN_URL
   value: https://auth-internal.domain.tld/auth
```
Please note that the TLD for the admin URL is different from the one on which we expose the deployment publicly.
The NGINX configuration snippet handling this setup is as follows:
```
http {
          resolver x.x.x.x;
          server {
              listen 443 ssl;
              server_name auth-internal.domain.tld;
              ssl_certificate     /path/to/ssl_cert;
              ssl_certificate_key /path/to/ssl_key;
              location /auth {
                proxy_pass         http://keycloak-service-name:8081/;
                proxy_set_header   Host $host;
                proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Host $host;
                proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto https;
                proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
              }
          }
      }
```
With this configuration, everything is working as expected. The admin UI is loading correctly, and Keycloak is accessible publicly and privately through the corresponding hostnames configured on the reverse proxies.

With `hostname:v2` we are unable to replicate this setup; specifically, we are having a hard time exposing Keycloak over a custom context path (`/auth` in the above case).
According to the [hostname v2 documentation](https://www.keycloak.org/server/hostname), everything can be set up using `X-Forwarded-*` headers through the reverse proxy, including the context path. This is how we set up those headers in our reverse proxy configuration:
```
location /auth {
  proxy_pass         http://keycloak-service-name:8180/;
  proxy_set_header   Host $host;
  proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Host $host;
  proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto https; 
  proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
  proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Prefix /auth;  # Please note that we're trying to use this header to set the context path 
}
```
Our new (ie. v26) Keycloak configuration relevant to what is being discussed in this issue is as follows:
```
- name: KC_HOSTNAME_STRICT
  value: 'false'
- name: KC_HTTP_ENABLED
  value: 'true'
- name: KC_HOSTNAME_BACKCHANNEL_DYNAMIC
  value: 'false'
- name: KC_PROXY_HEADERS
  value: xforwarded
```
When we deployed the above configurations, we hit an issue where we can't load the admin console, which seems to be hanging forever. By looking at the network console, we can see that the Keycloak UI is trying to load resources from a path that is missing the context path provided through the reverse proxy `X-Forwarded-Prefix` header.
Moreover, if we curl `https://auth-internal.domain.tld/auth/admin/master/console/` endpoint, we are returned a 200 status code (please note the `X-Forwarded-Prefix` header setting the context path being honored in the log below):
```
2024-11-25 16:39:29,809 INFO  [io.quarkus.http.access-log] (executor-thread-1) 
xx.xx.xx.xx - - [25/Nov/2024:16:39:29 +0000] "GET /auth/admin/master/console/ HTTP/1.0" 200 3189
```
and we can see the following in the HTML being returned:
```
<script id="environment" type="application/json">
      {
        "serverBaseUrl": "https://auth-internal.domain.tld",
        "adminBaseUrl": "https://auth-internal.domain.tld",
        "authUrl": "https://auth-internal.domain.tld",
        "authServerUrl": "https://auth-internal.domain.tld",
        "realm": "master",
        "clientId": "security-admin-console",
        "resourceUrl": "/resources/ihcaj/admin/keycloak.v2",
        "logo": "",
        "logoUrl": "",
        "consoleBaseUrl": "/admin/master/console/",
        "masterRealm": "master",
        "resourceVersion": "ihcaj"
      }
</script>
```
Please note that all the URLs are missing the context path specified through the header, which should have been appended to them.
All of this can be worked around by appending the build time option `--http-relative-path=/auth` in the Dockerfile for the Keycloak image, but we do not want to go down this route as this was achievable in the previous Keycloak versions. We need to use the image for multiple Keycloak deployments with different paths.

Thank you for your attention and for your precious work.

### Version

26.0.5

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Admin UI loading and the URLs in the description should have the context path provided by the reverse proxy appended to them.

### Actual behavior

Admin UI is not loading and the context path is not appended to URLs.

### How to Reproduce?

Please see the provided configuration snippets in the description for the issue.

### Anything else?

We even tried to add a `X-Forwarded-Port` to our reverse proxy configuration:
```
location /auth {
  proxy_pass         http://keycloak-service-name:8180/;
  proxy_set_header   Host $host;
  proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Host $host;
  proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto https;
  proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Port 666;        # Port added just to see if it made any difference
  proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
  proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Prefix /auth;  # Please note that we're trying to use this header to set the context path 
}
```
and we can see that it is being handled correctly:
```
<script id="environment" type="application/json">
  {
    "serverBaseUrl": "https://auth-internal.domain.tld:666",         # Looks like the header is being honored
    "adminBaseUrl": "https://auth-internal.domain.tld:666",
    "authUrl": "https://auth-internal.domain.tld:666",
    "authServerUrl": "https://auth-internal.domain.tld:666",
    "realm": "master",
    "clientId": "security-admin-console",
    "resourceUrl": "/resources/ihcaj/admin/keycloak.v2",
    "logo": "",
    "logoUrl": "",
    "consoleBaseUrl": "/admin/master/console/",
    "masterRealm": "master",
    "resourceVersion": "ihcaj"
  }
</script>
```

issue_title: Realm Roles Mapping Dropdown Hidden with Limited Permissions
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

The current implementation of Keycloak's role mapping user interface introduces a significant usability barrier when using fine-grained authorization, specifically preventing users with limited but legitimate role mapping permissions from effectively managing realm roles.

### Core Problem
The role mapping UI relies on the presence of client role permissions to render the role selection dropdown, creating a critical access bottleneck. This design flaw effectively locks out users who:
    - Have been explicitly granted map-role permissions for specific realm roles
    - Possess map-roles scope for users
    - Lack broader client role permissions

### Specific Mechanism of Failure
When a user lacks client role permissions, the UI fails in a cascading manner:
    - The client roles table appears empty
    - The dropdown mechanism for switching to realm roles remains hidden
    - Users cannot access the realm roles list, despite having explicit mapping permissions

### Permission Inconsistency
The bug reveals an inconsistent approach to permission handling:
    - Temporary grant of manage scope reveals the full role list
    - List refresh without page reload shows permitted realm roles
    - Page reload reverts to the restricted, non-functional state

### Version

26.0.6

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

1. UI should display a dropdown to switch between client and realm roles
2. Realm roles list should be accessible and filterable
3. User manager should see roles they have permission to map

**********************
as it shows in the following picture
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/35242107-c236-444c-8273-1f42efe9111b)

Note: I initially gained access to this list by assigning the manage scope to the user, which allowed visibility of all roles, including client roles. However, after revoking the manage scope and refreshing the list using the button above (without reloading the page), the list updated from the server to display only the realm roles for which the user has the map-role scope.

### Actual behavior

When a user lacks client role permissions, the UI fails in a cascading manner:
    - The client roles table appears empty
    - The dropdown mechanism for switching to realm roles remains hidden
    - Users cannot access the realm roles list, despite having explicit mapping permissions

### How to Reproduce?

1. Enable admin fine-grained authorization in Keycloak realm settings
2. Create a realm role named employee
3. Create a user manager with view users role
4. Configure fine-grained permissions:
          - Assign map-role scope permission for employee role to user manager
          - Assign map-roles scope permission for all users to user manager
5. login with manager user to admin console
6. Navigate to user management interface
7. Attempt to map roles to a user

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/549c3992-6e3a-40c9-b1ea-45a650cc8844)


### Anything else?

### Impact

      - Breaks expected fine-grained authorization workflows
      - Creates unnecessary administrative overhead
      - Prevents efficient user and role management
      - Reduces the utility of Keycloak's advanced permission models

This issue represents a critical UI/UX problem that directly impacts the usability of Keycloak's role management capabilities, particularly for organizations implementing strict, granular access control strategies.

issue_title: Token issuer is null in executeActionsEmail and sendVerifyEmail if no clientId is passed
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

I checked the implementation of the executeActionsEmail, especially https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/b20cfbbfa52c5636f9c3f46e525d8dff7aacb0c5/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/UserResource.java#L1120 and found out that the issuer for the generated `ExecuteActionsActionToken` is null, if no clientId is passed. 

It should take the clientId from the client found by https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/b20cfbbfa52c5636f9c3f46e525d8dff7aacb0c5/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/UserResource.java#L1134 and passing it to the SendEmailParams  `return new SendEmailParams(redirectUri, client.getClientId(), lifespan);`

Afterwards, the clientId is used in `result.clientId` to generate the token https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/b20cfbbfa52c5636f9c3f46e525d8dff7aacb0c5/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/UserResource.java#L905

This was introduced in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/02d64d959c088815fbb3809106d8967dd7524a81



### Version

26.0.6

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Token issuer is the clientId of the passed client or default client.

### Actual behavior

Token issuer is null if no clientId is passed

### How to Reproduce?

Call the api endpoint without passing a clientId and check the token.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Strange Random behavior - Intermittent missing organization claim in Keycloak JWT token
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

organizations

### Describe the bug

First, I do have  "organization:*" in my scope.

I use an OAuth2 client (remix-auth-oauth2) to authenticate like this : 
```
let strategy = new OAuth2Strategy(
  {
    clientId: OAUTH_CLIENT_ID,
    clientSecret: OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET,

    authorizationEndpoint: OAUTH_AUTH_ENDPOINT,
    tokenEndpoint: OAUTH_TOKEN_ENDPOINT,
    redirectURI: `http://localhost:5173${paths.callbacks.login}`,

    tokenRevocationEndpoint: OAUTH_REVOCATION_ENDPOINT,

    scopes: ["openid", "organization:*"],

  },
  async ({ tokens, request }) => {
    // here you can use the params above to get the user and return it
    // what you do inside this and how you find the user is up to you
    return await getUser(tokens, request);
  }
))
```

I tried to change the client, same behavior: 

The organization claim is inconsistently included in the JWT token returned by Keycloak, even when using the same scope parameters. This behavior is intermittent and does not follow a predictable pattern.

most of the time: 

```

{
   exp: 1732640581,
   iat: 1732640281,
   auth_time: 1732640281,
   jti: 'something',
   iss: 'http://localhost:8080/realms/test2',
   aud: 'account',
   sub: 'something',
   typ: 'Bearer',
   azp: 'something',
   sid: 'something',
   acr: '1',
   'allowed-origins': [ 'http://localhost:5173' ],
   realm_access: {
     roles: [ 'offline_access', 'default-roles-test2', 'uma_authorization' ]
   },
   resource_access: { account: { roles: [Array] } },
   scope: 'openid organization:* email profile organization',
   email_verified: true,
   organization: {
    test2: { id: 'something' },
     test3: { id: 'something' },
     test: {
       something: [Array],
       something2: [Array],
       id: 'something'
     }
   },
   name: 'First Last',
   preferred_username: 'test',
   given_name: 'First',
   family_name: 'Last',
   email: 'test@example.com'
 }
```

Sometimes:

```
 {
   exp: 1732640594,
   iat: 1732640294,
  auth_time: 1732640294,
   jti: 'something',
   iss: 'http://localhost:8080/realms/test2',
   aud: 'account',
   sub: 'something',
   typ: 'Bearer',
   azp: 'nest-app',
   sid: 'something',
   acr: '1',
   'allowed-origins': [ 'http://localhost:5173' ],
   realm_access: {
     roles: [ 'offline_access', 'default-roles-test2', 'uma_authorization' ]
   },
   resource_access: { account: { roles: [Array] } },
   scope: 'openid email profile organization organization:*',
   email_verified: true,
   name: 'First Last',
   preferred_username: 'test',
   given_name: 'First',
   family_name: 'Last',
   email: 'test@example.com'
 }
```
It appears really randomly, like one time out of 20.


The only things who seem to be different is the order in scope : 
- For working requests : `'openid organization:* email profile organization'` 
- For non working requests: `'openid email profile organization organization:*'`

Thanks a lot for your help ! :) 

### Version

26.0.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The organization claim should consistently appear in the JWT token if the request and configuration are unchanged and good scope.

### Actual behavior

The organization claim appears intermittently in the response token, even when the request and Keycloak configuration remain the same.



### How to Reproduce?

see above

### Anything else?

_No response_
